message:
fix(fr-command): MEMORY DOCTOR / LOLWUT / CLUSTER SLAVES match upstream wire format

- MEMORY DOCTOR (br-frankenredis-yj7h): the canned single-line
  "Sam, I have no memory problems" reply now mirrors upstream
  object.c::getMemoryDoctorReport. fr does not track allocated
  bytes (no jemalloc, no zmalloc_used) so the empty branch is
  selected by a key-count proxy: dbsize >= 50,000 keys triggers
  the "I can't find any memory issue" path; otherwise the verbose
  empty-instance "Hi Sam, this instance is empty..." text is
  emitted, byte-for-byte identical to vendored redis 7.2.4.
  Verbose multi-bullet "issues" branch (peak/frag/RSS/script-cache)
  is left as a follow-up — fr does not yet surface those metrics.

- LOLWUT (br-frankenredis-m5nf): replaces the bespoke 12-line ASCII
  art generator with upstream's lolwutUnstableCommand body —
  "Redis ver. <REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION>\n". Matches vendored Redis
  byte-for-byte modulo the patch number (fr reports 7.2.0 from
  fr_store::REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION, vendored is 7.2.4). VERSION 5/6
  arguments are accepted (so clients don't error out) but emit
  the same single-line body — Schotter / braille art ports stay
  as future work.

- CLUSTER SLAVES (br-frankenredis-gcmq): adds SLAVES as the
  upstream-deprecated alias for REPLICAS in fr-command::cluster_cmd.
  Previously fr returned "ERR unknown subcommand 'SLAVES'" while
  upstream emits "ERR This instance has cluster support disabled"
  on a non-cluster instance (and the actual replica array on a
  cluster instance, mirroring REPLICAS). Reference:
  legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/cluster-slaves.json.

Updates core_server.json (memory_doctor + memory_doctor_duplicate_block)
and core_connection.json (lolwut_returns_string + lolwut_with_version)
fixtures to the new upstream-aligned bodies. Adds a focused
fr-command regression test for LOLWUT VERSION dispatch.

Found by ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored
Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) on a small command battery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
